methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Bone AI is a Seed-stage frontier-tech company in Defense & Security founded by DK Lee. The company has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety—all operating in the same sector at Seed or adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $12M raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage, implying a $30M valuation. Peer-Median analysis examines four same-sector comparables: Tenna Systems ($60M, Seed), Ares Industries ($150M, Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M, Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M, Series A). The median across this peer set is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $26M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $128M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful stage and maturity differences within the peer set; the Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while Peer-Median captures the upper-end valuations achieved by Series A companies. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, intellectual property defensibility, and customer concentration—factors not reflected in stage or comparable peer multiples alone.
